title: Virgin Galactic Announces Fourth Quarter and Full Year 2025 Financial Results and Provides Business Update
text: Virgin Galactic released its financial results for the fourth quarter and full year 2025, highlighting progress toward commercial spaceflight operations. The company maintained a strong cash position of $338 million as of December 31, 2025. For the full year, revenue declined to $2 million from $7 million in 2024, primarily due to pausing commercial flights to focus on new SpaceShip production. Operating expenses decreased to $287 million (GAAP) compared to $384 million previously. The net loss improved to $279 million from $347 million in 2024. Free cash flow was negative $438 million for 2025 versus negative $475 million in 2024. The company generated $122 million through share issuances during the year. CEO Michael Colglazier stated: With assembly of our first SpaceShip nearly complete and ground testing set to begin in April, we have released a limited number of Virgin Galactic Spaceflight Expeditions, each priced at $750,000. The first new SpaceShip will enter ground testing in April, with flight tests beginning in Q3 2026. Commercial operations are targeted for Q4 2026. A second SpaceShip is expected between late Q4 2026 and early Q1 2027. The company also executed a capital realignment reducing debt obligations by $142 million.
